name: @radzor/rss-feed
description: Parse, generate, and watch RSS 2.0 and Atom feeds. Supports polling for new items, feed generation from structured data, and event-driven notifications when new content appears.

inputs.name: userAgent
inputs.description: User-Agent header sent when fetching feeds.

inputs.name: defaultPollIntervalMs
inputs.description: Default polling interval in milliseconds for watched feeds.

inputs.name: maxItems
inputs.description: Maximum number of items to return when parsing a feed.

outputs.name: feedData
outputs.description: Parsed feed data including metadata and items.

fields.name: items
fields.type: Array<{ title: string; link: string; description?: string; pubDate?: string; guid?: string; author?: string }>

fields.name: lastUpdated
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last feed update.

fields.name: feedUrl
fields.description: The URL the feed was fetched from.

events.name: onNewItem
events.description: Fired when a watched feed has a new item that wasn't present in the previous poll.

actions.name: parse

params.name: urlOrXml
params.description: A feed URL to fetch, or raw XML/Atom string to parse.

actions.description: Parse an RSS/Atom feed from a URL or raw XML string.

actions.name: generate

params.name: options
params.description: Feed metadata and items for generating an RSS 2.0 XML string.

actions.description: Generate an RSS 2.0 XML feed from structured data.

actions.name: watch

params.name: intervalMs
params.description: Poll interval in milliseconds. Defaults to defaultPollIntervalMs.

actions.description: Start polling a feed URL for new items. New items trigger onNewItem events.

actions.name: unwatch

params.name: url
params.description: The feed URL to stop watching.

actions.description: Stop watching a feed URL. Returns true if the feed was being watched.

connectsTo.description: Process new RSS items through AI or notify channels

llm.constraints: Uses native fetch (Node.js 18+). Parses RSS 2.0 and Atom feeds using basic XML parsing (no external XML library). Feed watching uses setInterval-based polling — not suitable for hundreds of concurrent watches.
